summary: Ukrainian forces launched a massive Shahed swarm attack, downing 102 drones but still hitting 12 targets across nine locations. Significant strikes hit Russian naval assets near Novorossiysk, an oil terminal, and the 960th assault aviation regiment in Primorsko‑Akhtarsk, disrupting Su‑25 launch capabilities. Civilian casualties rose in Starobelsk, highlighting continued targeting of infrastructure and population centers.
key_points: A coordinated Shahed swarm of 124 UAVs attacked from multiple directions, Ukrainian air defenses downed 102, but 12 drones struck nine locations across the north, south and east (Source: @kpszsu/62474).
Ukrainian UAVs reportedly struck the Russian frigate Admiral Essen and a Project 1239 missile ship near the Novorossiysk naval base in Krasnodar region (Source: @eRadarrua/93134).
An oil terminal "Grushova" near Novorossiysk, a major Russian fuel hub, was attacked by Ukrainian UAVs on 22 May 2026 (Source: @eRadarrua/93103).

A partisan attack on the 960th assault aviation regiment in Primorsko‑Akhtarsk caused a large fire and disabled infrastructure supporting Su‑25 sorties and Shahed launch sites (Source: @eRadarrua/93130).

Ukrainian strike groups were reported operating in Poltava, Sumy, Kharkiv, Cherkasy and Chernihiv oblasts, moving towards key settlements such as Piryatin, Romodan, Mala Danylivka, Smila and Lhiv (Source: @eRadarrua/93117).
Civilian casualties increased as attacks on the Starobelsk Pedagogical College in LNR killed 12 children and left nine trapped under rubble (Source: @tass_agency/377210).

Multiple Shahed detections were reported near Vinogradar (towards Syrts), Bucha (towards Kyiv) and Lukyanivka/Shulyavka (towards Solomyanka) indicating continued low‑altitude UAV activity (Source: @mon1tor_ua/66911, @mon1tor_ua/66900, @mon1tor_ua/66912).
Acoustic radar recorded UAV activity from Svytoshyn to Karavaiv Dachi in Vidradny, confirming expanded UAV surveillance coverage (Source: @mon1tor_ua/66904).

sections.executive_summary: A coordinated Shahed swarm attack resulted in 102 drones being shot down but still inflicted damage at nine sites, underscoring Ukraine's growing UAV capability. Ukrainian strikes hit high‑value Russian naval and logistical targets near Novorossiysk and crippled the 960th assault aviation regiment’s Su‑25 launch infrastructure in Primorsko‑Akhtarsk. Civilian toll rose sharply after a deadly attack on a college in Starobelsk, highlighting the conflict’s expanding humanitarian impact.

points: Ukrainian strike groups were active in Poltava, Sumy, Kharkiv, Cherkasy and Chernihiv oblasts, advancing towards Piryatin, Romodan, Mala Danylivka, Smila and Lhiv, indicating coordinated multi‑directional offensives (Source: @eRadarrua/93117).

UAV detections over Vinogradar (Shahed heading towards Syrts), Bucha (Shahed towards Kyiv) and Lukyanivka/Shulyavka (Shahed towards Solomyanka) show expanded low‑altitude attack vectors across central Ukraine (Source: @mon1tor_ua/66911, @mon1tor_ua/66900, @mon1tor_ua/66912).
Acoustic radar logged UAV movement from Svytoshyn to Karavaiv Dachi in Vidradny, confirming enhanced surveillance and early‑warning capabilities (Source: @mon1tor_ua/66904).

UAVs were observed over Chernihiv region near Nosivka and over Kharkiv heading towards Stary Saltiv and Pechenigi, indicating pressure on northern and eastern fronts (Source: @kpszsu/62483, @kpszsu/62480).

Ukrainian forces claimed successful strikes on Russian naval assets near Novorossiysk, including the Admiral Essen frigate and a Project 1239 missile ship, demonstrating reach into Russian rear areas (Source: @eRadarrua/93134).
Russian naval facilities at Novorossiysk suffered UAV attacks on the "Grushova" oil terminal, a key fuel hub for southern Russia (Source: @eRadarrua/93103).
The 960th assault aviation regiment in Primorsko‑Akhtarsk was hit by partisan explosives, igniting a fire that disabled Su‑25 launch pads and Shahed launch infrastructure (Source: @eRadarrua/93130).
Civilian infrastructure in the LNR was struck, with an attack on Starobelsk Pedagogical College killing 12 children and trapping nine under debris (Source: @tass_agency/377210).
Russian air defense reported downing 102 hostile UAVs during a large‑scale Shahed swarm, but acknowledged 12 penetrations, reflecting strain on defensive systems (Source: @kpszsu/62474).
Ukrainian UAV activity was detected near Russian‑controlled areas of Kursk and Belgorod, though no confirmed damage was reported in these messages.
Ukrainian UAVs over Kharkiv region moved towards Stary Saltiv and Pechenigi, targeting supply routes and forward positions (Source: @kpszsu/62480).
Shahed swarms approached from Kursk, Shatalovo, Bryansk and Primorsko‑Akhtarsk, striking eastern sectors and testing Ukrainian air defenses (Source: @kpszsu/62474).
Ukrainian strike groups in Sumy (direction unspecified) indicate continued pressure on the northern edge of the eastern front (Source: @eRadarrua/93117).
UAV sightings over Chernihiv near Nosivka suggest Ukrainian reconnaissance and harassment of Russian rear areas (Source: @kpszsu/62483).
UAV activity reported moving from Poltava towards Piryatin and Romodan, potentially supporting eastern thrusts (Source: @eRadarrua/93117).
Acoustic detection from Svytoshyn to Karavaiv Dachi in Vidradny confirms UAV presence near the northern Kyiv region (Source: @mon1tor_ua/66904).
Shahed drones were observed near Vinogradar heading towards Syrts, indicating pressure on northern supply lines (Source: @mon1tor_ua/66911).
UAVs detected over Chernihiv region near Nosivka, maintaining pressure on northern Russian‑aligned positions (Source: @kpszsu/62483).
Large‑scale Shahed attack included vectors from Kursk and Bryansk, testing northern air defense layers (Source: @kpszsu/62474).
UAVs reported over Poltava moving towards Piryatin, a strategic node linking northern and central fronts (Source: @eRadarrua/93117).
UAV strike on the "Grushova" oil terminal near Novorossiysk disrupted a major fuel supply hub for Russian forces in the south (Source: @eRadarrua/93103).
Partisan attack on the 960th assault aviation regiment in Primorsko‑Akhtarsk disabled Su‑25 launch capabilities, affecting southern air support (Source: @eRadarrua/93130).
Shahed swarms originated from Primorsko‑Akhtarsk and other southern directions, indicating coordinated southern pressure (Source: @kpszsu/62474).
UAV detections near Bucha heading towards Kyiv suggest southern corridor activity toward the capital (Source: @mon1tor_ua/66900).
UAV activity reported from Shatalovo (south‑west of Moscow) may impact logistics routes feeding the southern front (Source: @kpszsu/62474).
Ukrainian air defenses shot down 102 of 124 attacking Shahed, Gerbera, Italmas and imitation drones, but 12 hostile UAVs still struck nine locations (Source: @kpszsu/62474).
UAV attacks from multiple vectors (Kursk, Shatalovo, Bryansk, Millerevo, Primorsko‑Akhtarsk, Gardievske) demonstrate expanded Russian airspace penetration (Source: @kpszsu/62474).
Ukrainian strike groups employed UAVs for precision targeting of logistics nodes in Poltava, Sumy, Kharkiv, Cherkasy and Chernihiv (Source: @eRadarrua/93117).
Russian naval and oil infrastructure near Novorossiysk faced UAV strikes, indicating Ukrainian capability to threaten strategic air‑defended assets (Source: @eRadarrua/93103, @eRadarrua/93134).
Civilian airspace over LNR experienced UAV attacks on educational facilities, raising concerns about UAV rules of engagement (Source: @tass_agency/377210).

key_developments: Ukrainian forces launched a 124‑drone Shahed swarm, downing 102 but still hitting 12 targets across nine sites, showing both capability and vulnerability (Source: @kpszsu/62474).
UAVs struck the Russian frigate Admiral Essen and a Project 1239 missile ship near Novorossiysk, indicating successful deep‑strike operations (Source: @eRadarrua/93134).
The "Grushova" oil terminal near Novorossiysk was attacked, potentially disrupting fuel supplies for Russian southern forces (Source: @eRadarrua/93103).
A partisan explosion at the 960th assault aviation regiment in Primorsko‑Akhtarsk disabled Su‑25 launch infrastructure, reducing Russian air support in the region (Source: @eRadarrua/93130).
Ukrainian strike groups operated in five oblasts, advancing towards Piryatin, Romodan, Mala Danylivka, Smila and Lhiv, suggesting coordinated multi‑axis offensives (Source: @eRadarrua/93117).
Civilian casualties rose in Starobelsk as an attack on a college killed 12 children and left nine trapped, highlighting intensified targeting of civilian sites (Source: @tass_agency/377210).
Multiple Shahed detections near Vinogradar, Bucha and Lukyanivka demonstrate persistent low‑altitude UAV threats to central Ukraine (Source: @mon1tor_ua/66911, @mon1tor_ua/66900, @mon1tor_ua/66912).
sections.threat_assessment: Ukrainian UAV operations have escalated, achieving deep strikes on Russian naval assets and critical infrastructure while sustaining significant attrition. Russian air defenses remain strained, downing the majority of incoming drones but still allowing penetrations that damage logistics and civilian targets. The emergence of coordinated partisan attacks on airbases suggests growing internal resistance that could further degrade Russian air capabilities.
sections.regional_response: Ukrainian command appears to be integrating UAV swarms with ground strike groups across multiple fronts, leveraging real‑time radar and acoustic detection to guide attacks. Russian forces are reinforcing air defense zones around strategic hubs like Novorossiysk and Kursk, yet the persistence of successful strikes indicates gaps in layered defense. Local authorities in contested regions are increasing civilian alert protocols following the rise in civilian casualties.
sections.intelligence_notes: Future monitoring should focus on UAV launch corridors from Kursk, Shatalovo and Primorsko‑Akhtarsk, as well as the operational status of the 960th regiment's replacement capabilities. Satellite imagery of Novorossiysk's oil terminal will verify damage extent. Tracking acoustic radar feeds in central Ukraine can provide early warning of low‑altitude UAV incursions.
